domain: a software engineer's work and personal life

turns.text: First day at Veridian Systems went well. I got staffed on Project Atlas, working alongside a teammate named Dana.
Congrats on starting at Veridian Systems. Project Atlas with Dana sounds like a strong start.
My manager for Project Atlas is named Rourke. He runs the weekly architecture reviews.
Good to know Rourke leads the architecture reviews on Atlas.
Big news at home — I adopted a cat. Her name is Mochi and she's a Ragdoll.
Welcome home, Mochi the Ragdoll! That's wonderful.
Slow Sunday. I reorganized my bookshelf and finally finished that sci-fi trilogy I'd been stuck on for months.
Sounds like a satisfying reset day. Nice to close out a long trilogy.
I booked a trip to Lisbon with my friend Sofia for the spring. Really looking forward to it.
Lisbon in spring with Sofia — that should be a lovely trip.
Honestly I prefer coffee over tea, and I do my best focused work first thing in the morning.
Coffee and early mornings — noted as your peak setup.
I started rock climbing this month. My coach is a guy named Theo and he's really patient.
Rock climbing with a patient coach like Theo is a great way to start.
Spent the weekend repotting my houseplants. The monstera has gotten huge and needed a bigger pot.
Repotting day! A thriving monstera is a good problem to have.
Great news on the team — Dana moved up to Staff Engineer this week after the last review cycle.
That's a well-earned step up for Dana. Congrats to the whole team.
Tried a new ramen place downtown called Tanuki. The tonkotsu broth was incredible.
Tanuki sounds like a keeper — great tonkotsu is hard to find.
Rough night. I had to rush Mochi to the emergency vet for a blocked bladder, but she's stable now.
I'm so sorry — scary night. Glad Mochi is stable after the emergency visit.
My sister is getting married in October and asked me to give a speech at the reception.
That's a real honor. October will come fast — exciting times.
Caught up with Sofia today. She just started a new role at Brightwave Capital as an analyst.
Big move for Sofia — analyst at Brightwave Capital sounds promising.
I've been getting into film photography. Picked up an old Pentax and shot my first roll of black and white.
Film photography is a rewarding rabbit hole. That Pentax should be fun.
Theo suggested I switch over to Summit Ridge — apparently the bouldering walls there are way better.
If Theo rates Summit Ridge, it's probably worth the move for the bouldering.
My landlord finally fixed the leaky kitchen faucet that had been dripping for weeks.
Relief at last — a dripping faucet is maddening. Glad it's sorted.
Rourke made the call in standup today — we're standardizing all new services on Go from now on.
A clear direction from Rourke. Standardizing the stack should reduce friction.
Ran my first 10K race this morning. Slower than I hoped but I finished without walking.
Finishing a 10K without walking is a real milestone. Well done.
Over lunch Dana mentioned she used to be at Cobalt Robotics before all this.
Interesting background — Cobalt Robotics explains a lot of her instincts.
I baked sourdough for the first time. The crust came out great but the crumb was a bit dense.
First sourdough with a great crust is a win — the crumb improves with practice.

queries.question: What is the current job title of Dana, who I work with on Project Atlas?
What breed is Mochi, the one I rushed to the emergency vet?
Which company now employs Sofia, my travel companion for the Lisbon trip?
Which gym did my climbing coach Theo recommend I train at?
Which programming language did my Project Atlas manager Rourke choose for the team?
Where did my Project Atlas teammate Dana work before joining Veridian Systems?
Do I prefer tea or coffee?
Who is my rock climbing coach?
What city did I book a trip to?
